scores.fix: Add the cross-class higher-budget sweep (already named as the natural significance gate) or demote the ordering claim one register in abstract and conclusion.
scores.justification: Method discipline is exemplary: the reduction, the pre-registered k* class test with exact and permutation p-values and an explicit power floor, and the framing "we treat the analytical NE characterization as ground truth and the PPO trainability sweep as a falsification test, in that order" (— §1) are all sound; the β-inertness correction honestly downgrades a prior confirmation to a solver-noise probe. Held at 5/6 because the central §4 correlation rests on a 4-seed, 50-iteration budget whose class separations the paper itself concedes are ordering-only, so the headline trainability claim is structurally under-powered relative to its prominence.

scores.fix: Re-derive the metric-robustness sentence against the n=20-merged artifact (or a pinned snapshot) and report class-mean CIs instead of bare orderings.
scores.justification: Evidence volume is strong (148-run sweep, 20-seed buy-down, 4x/16x ladders, 10k-episode paired batteries, 12/12 equilibrium coverage) and every spot-checked number traces to a committed artifact. But the robustness sentence "the load-bearing claim is robust to the metric choice" (— §4) rests on a 0.001 class-mean separation, and recomputing class means from the current committed recalibrated_verdict.json (regenerated 2026-07-04 with n=20 for 8 cells) yields hom means of 0.041 (symmetric) vs 0.046 (mixed) — the claimed ordering inverts on the freshest committed data.

scores.fix: Compress the abstract to ~250 words; move the per-test statistics to their body sections.
scores.justification: The §1 Contributions paragraph enumerates (1)-(5) crisply, including the double negative result: "we retire two intuitive predictors in sequence and identify the honest remaining signal" (— §1). Deducted one point because the abstract has grown to roughly a full rendered page carrying dozens of statistics, so the one-sentence-per-item extraction the rubric asks of the abstract now only works from §1, not from the abstract itself.

scores.fix: Add a short paragraph engaging OpenSpiel/exploitability-based evaluation and qualify the only-published claim; re-run pub-litsearch.
scores.justification: The six-benchmark comparison is honest — the paper "dominates on exactly one column---NE characterisability---and loses on every other" (— §Related work) — but the claim "it is the only published parametric MARL game where the question" of right-equilibrium convergence admits a ground-truth answer (— Abstract) is never positioned against the small-game equilibrium-computation and exploitability-evaluation tradition (OpenSpiel; PSRO/NashConv-style convergence-to-equilibrium metrics), which is the closest prior line for exactly that evaluative question. No litsearch sibling exists, so the legacy baseline applies; the gap is a real positioning miss, not just a missing bonus.

scores.fix: Pin the paper to a versioned snapshot of recalibrated_verdict.json (commit hash or frozen copy) or regenerate Figure 2 and the §4 numbers from the merged data.
scores.justification: The apparatus is unusually strong (pip wheel, frozen scenario IDs, parity manifest, per-cell baselines, PAPER_RESULTS.md; all 16 spot-checked artifact paths exist). Deducted because the claim that the v2 recalibrated artifacts "reproduce byte-for-byte from the committed per-seed summaries" (— §Discussion, Reproducibility) is stale: the committed recalibrated_verdict.json was regenerated on 2026-07-04 (commit a5b8ccdc) with n=20 means for 8 cells, so re-running figures/src/recalibrated_heatmap.py today produces a Figure 2 that differs from the shipped one (e.g. 0.372 vs 0.043 at the kappa=0.3, c=1.0, beta=0.5 cell); the HuggingFace baselines pathway also remains in progress.

scores.fix: Rewrite the unsampled-cell description at all three sites to name kappa in {0.3, 0.7} at c=0.5, fix the Table 1 caption row-total sentence, and make Table 3 paths line-breakable.
scores.justification: Both figures are self-contained and readable, but the headline Figure 1 caption carries a factual error: "the six unsampled cells (high-$\kappa\times c{=}0.5$ corner, subsumed by the $c{=}1.0$ row) are hatched" (— Fig. 1 caption) — the actual unsampled cells, verified against experiments/nash/phase_diagram/results.json, are the kappa in {0.3, 0.7} columns of the c=0.5 panel, which are neither high-kappa nor a corner; the same wrong phrase recurs in §3 body text, and the Table 1 caption compounds it ("the row totals are 9 except at $\kappa=0.7$" — the kappa=0.3 row also has 6 cells). Table 3's provenance column and caption overflow the right margin (98-105pt overfull).

scores.fix: Break or footnote the long \texttt artifact paths (\path, \seqsplit, or move provenance to a table footnote) and re-check the log for zero overfulls above ~10pt.
scores.justification: Structure and flow are intact and the full pdflatex+bibtex cycle completes with zero unresolved references, but the build logs 10 overfull hboxes of which five are severe (96.7-128.9pt, i.e. up to ~4.5cm of text past the right margin, visually confirmed on rendered pages 10-12), mostly from long inline artifact paths in §5 such as the anchors paragraph following "A single night consists of seven sequential phases applied in a fixed, load-bearing order" is clean but §5's long \texttt paths are not; the rubric names overfull hboxes in body sections explicitly.

All 9 cite keys resolve against refs.bib and every entry carries complete author/title/venue/year fields; the final compile pass reports no instance of an undefined citation found, and no instance of an unsourced load-bearing external claim found — repo-internal results cite committed artifact paths per the repo convention. The Appendix A named-template attributions without cites remain a camera-ready polish item, not a hygiene defect.

scores.fix: One de-duplication pass: state each caveat once in its owning section, cut the abstract to ~250 words, and halve the conclusion.
scores.justification: The v7-v9 accretion has real bloat: the abstract alone now runs ~700 words (statistics like "A $4{\times}$/$16{\times}$ budget-scaling ladder sharpens the hardness statement" (— Abstract) belong in §5, not the abstract), the conclusion restates the abstract at comparable length, and the 26-page artifact serves a 4-page workshop brief. A busy reviewer can no longer extract the contribution in 90 seconds from the abstract; the caveat discipline is admirable but many caveats are stated three or four times (abstract, §1, section body, conclusion).

findings.rationale: The committed experiments/p3_specialization/phase_diagram_ppo_v2/recalibrated_verdict.json was regenerated 2026-07-04 (commit a5b8ccdc) with n=20 means for 8 buy-down cells; the paper's headline class means (0.180/0.107/0.059/-0.049), Figure 2 cell values, and the byte-for-byte reproduction claim no longer re-derive from it (current-artifact recomputation gives 0.142/0.096/0.060 on gap_closed_ne).
findings.suggested_fix: Pin a frozen snapshot of the artifact (hash or copy) or regenerate Figure 2 and the section-4 class means from the merged data.

findings.rationale: The metric-robustness sentence (homogeneous ordering symmetric 0.051 > mixed 0.050) inverts under the current committed artifact (0.041 vs 0.046), so the robust-to-metric-choice claim is contradicted by the freshest committed data.
findings.suggested_fix: Re-derive the robustness sentence against the updated artifact and report class-mean CIs.

findings.rationale: The six unsampled Nash cells are the kappa in {0.3, 0.7} columns of the c=0.5 panel (verified vs results.json), but Figure 1's caption, the section-3 body, and Table 1's caption all describe them as the 'high-kappa x c=0.5 corner' and 'row totals are 9 except at kappa=0.7'.
findings.suggested_fix: Correct the unsampled-cell description at all three sites.

findings.rationale: Five severe overfull hboxes (96.7-128.9pt) put text visibly into the right margin on rendered pages 4 and 10-12, driven by long inline \texttt artifact paths added in v7-v9.
findings.suggested_fix: Make the long artifact paths line-breakable or move them to footnotes/table notes.

findings.rationale: related-work: the 'only published parametric MARL game' claim (abstract, related work, conclusion) is never positioned against the OpenSpiel / exploitability-evaluation (NashConv, PSRO) tradition, the closest prior line for measuring convergence-to-equilibrium quality; no litsearch sibling exists to substantiate the survey.
findings.suggested_fix: related-work: re-run pub-litsearch targeting equilibrium-computation benchmarks and exploitability-based MARL evaluation; engage or qualify the claim.

findings.rationale: The abstract runs ~700 words (a full rendered page) and the conclusion restates it at comparable length; caveats repeat at up to four sites.
findings.suggested_fix: Cut the abstract to ~250 words and de-duplicate caveats to their owning sections.

findings.rationale: Carry-forward from the v6 review: Appendix A's named game-theory templates (Volunteer's Dilemma, Public Goods, Stag Hunt) still ship without cite keys.
findings.suggested_fix: Add one canonical citation per named template for the camera-ready.

findings.rationale: Carry-forward from the v6 review: the small-q(k) Taylor linearisation in Appendix B.2 is still not named among the 'Five sources of systematic bias' in B.5.
findings.suggested_fix: Name the linearisation as a sixth bias source or footnote it at the Taylor step.

findings.rationale: HuggingFace baselines pathway is still 'in progress and not yet complete' (unchanged since v6).
findings.suggested_fix: Update the status for the camera-ready.
